description: Critical wildfire coverage regions requiring IoT sensor deployment and monitoring. Prioritized by current risk, population downwind impact, and existing coverage gaps.

regions.name: Ontario–Michigan Border Zone
regions.why_critical: Source zone of July 2026 smoke event choking all of Michigan and Northeast US. Ash fall confirmed in Upper Peninsula July 15, 2026. Zero IoT ground sensor coverage.
regions.notes: Straddles US-Canada border — requires CIFFC + USFS joint deployment agreement

regions.name: Northern Ontario Boreal Forest
regions.why_critical: Largest active fire zone in Canada as of July 2026. Boreal forest stores massive carbon — fires here drive continental-scale PM2.5 events.
regions.notes: Vast remote terrain — satellite + drone coverage essential; ground sensors via helicopter deployment

regions.name: Upper Peninsula, Michigan
regions.why_critical: Ash fall confirmed July 15, 2026. Dense forest, sparse population, no IoT coverage. Gateway for Canadian fire smoke into Lower Michigan.
regions.notes: Michigan EGLE partnership opportunity — state agency already engaged on air quality alerts

regions.name: Alberta / BC Interior
regions.why_critical: Recurring megafire origin zone. 2023 fires here triggered US-wide smoke events. Minimal sensor coverage across vast boreal and mountain terrain.
regions.notes: BC Wildfire Service partnership — they have existing infrastructure to build on

regions.name: Saskatchewan Boreal Forest
regions.why_critical: High-risk boreal zone with zero IoT coverage. Fires here travel east and south, impacting Great Plains and Great Lakes states.
regions.notes: Saskatchewan Ministry of Environment partnership needed

regions.name: Northern Minnesota / Boundary Waters
regions.why_critical: Active fires July 2026 contributing to Michigan smoke event. BWCA is a beloved wilderness area — fires here devastate tourism and ecology.
regions.notes: Minnesota DNR partnership — BWCA has existing ranger infrastructure

regions.name: Pacific Northwest (WA/OR)
regions.why_critical: Annual megafire zone. Cascade Range fires impact Seattle, Portland, and drive smoke events across the continent.
regions.notes: WA DNR and OR ODF have existing camera networks — integration opportunity

regions.name: Northern Rockies (MT/ID)
regions.why_critical: High fuel load, remote terrain, recurring large fires. Gateway to Great Plains smoke transport.
regions.notes: USFS Northern Region has existing infrastructure

regions.name: New Mexico / Arizona Highlands
regions.why_critical: Extreme drought conditions. 2022 Hermits Peak/Calf Canyon fire was largest in NM history. Recurring risk.
regions.notes: Tribal lands overlap — Pueblo and Navajo Nation partnership critical

regions.name: Quebec Boreal Forest
regions.why_critical: 2023 megafire origin — drove historic smoke events across US East Coast. Zero IoT coverage in vast boreal zone.
regions.notes: SOPFEU (Quebec wildfire agency) partnership — they are well-organized and receptive

regions.name: Great Lakes National Forests
regions.why_critical: Downwind impact zone for 30M+ people. Ottawa, Hiawatha, Chequamegon-Nicolet National Forests. Increasing fire risk with climate change.
regions.notes: USFS Eastern Region partnership — lower immediate fire risk but massive downwind population
